A1.guidance: Production MUST be a guided writing task with sentence frames provided. Ask the student to write 3-5 sentences using new vocabulary or structures from this lesson. Guided writing is appropriate and achievable even at A1. Alternatively, a guided dialogue (EO-01) with fully scripted turns and 2 pre-given response options per turn is appropriate when the session focus is oral production.

Vocabulary lists with translations are presentation, not production
Vocabulary matching is presentation/practice, not production
Semantic categorisation is presentation/practice, not production
Reading comprehension is reception, not production
Ludic activities are not production tasks

levelSpecificNotes.note: Provide a full model sentence for each frame; student fills in only the target vocabulary or structure
Sentence frames must supply the subject and verb; student completes the complement only
Guided dialogue must be fully scripted at A1; student chooses from 2 pre-given responses per turn

A2.guidance: Guided writing with a model paragraph provided. Student writes 4-6 sentences using the target grammar and vocabulary. A short opinion sentence or simple description. Scaffolding phrase starters optional. A short guided dialogue (EO-01 or EO-08, 2-3 turns) is a valid alternative when the session focus is oral production.

levelSpecificNotes.note: Description tasks at A2 must provide an image or explicit topic prompt; 3-5 sentences maximum
Free conversation at A2 is a short exchange on a familiar topic; 2-3 turns only

B1.guidance: Production must be a communicative task: an opinion paragraph, a short role-play scenario description, or a problem-solving task where the student uses new language in a meaningful context. 80-120 words.

levelSpecificNotes.note: Opinion paragraph at B1: 80-120 words; student states a position and gives 2 reasons; no formal essay structure required
Open role-play at B1 must have a clear communicative goal (buy a ticket, make a complaint) and a 3-4 turn minimum expectation

B2.guidance: Structured argument, formal email, or extended communicative task using the target register. 150-200 words. Authentic stimulus (text or question) may be used as springboard.

levelSpecificNotes.note: Formal email at B2 must specify register constraints (formal vs semi-formal) and functional goal (complaint, request, enquiry)
Oral mediation at B2: student summarises a 100-150 word text for a non-specialist; 3-4 minutes speaking time

C1.guidance: open-ended task requiring autonomous extended language use: a structured argument, a creative writing piece, or a task requiring register and pragmatic awareness. 200+ words.

levelSpecificNotes.note: Register transformation at C1: student rewrites a formal text as informal and vice versa, maintaining full propositional content
Negotiation at C1: open-ended scenario with conflicting interests; student must reach a compromise using pragmatic strategies

C2.guidance: Essay, debate position, mediation task, or creative writing requiring near-native register control. 200+ words. Student selects appropriate register and genre without prompting.

Creative writing at C2 may be unstructured; student chooses genre, register, and narrative voice autonomously
